scope_note: This v0.6 addition previews a staged, PROPOSED / NON-CANONICAL v2.0 (docs r1) two-pathway candidate for the wind_tornado_wind cell -- straight_line_convective and tornado_direct_hit -- evaluated as mutually-exclusive ordered damage states on a single WT_TURBINE_EQUIPMENT_ASSEMBLY atom ($1,090,000/MW). It does NOT alter, supersede, or downgrade the v0.5 RESOLVED_FROM_DAMAGE_MODELING crosswalk, which remains built against the canonical, PROMOTED wind_tornado_wind@model_v1_0__docs_r4 cell.
golden_rule_on_grain: A source with a wind loss number is NOT a benchmark until its sub-peril frame, denominator, coverage, asset class, geography, and loss basis are understood. v2's turbine-equipment atom is a DIFFERENT denominator than v1.0's five-unit physical base; every OLD_VS_NEW row is flagged different_denominators_do_not_treat_as_regression_target and must never be treated as a regression target for v1.0.

upstream_cell_governance.v1_0_status: promoted; canonical_runtime_artifact=true; UNCHANGED by this addition

v2_0_docs_r1_candidate.note: Deliberately absent from the artifact index and portable library v2.5

upstream_cell_governance.curve_form_v2: ordered_damage_state_lognormal: Q_j(x)=Phi(ln(x/theta_j)/beta_ln); DR = P1*c1 + P2*c2 + P3*c3

blank_cell_examples: damage_curve_intensity_reference_v2_proposed.csv row straight_line_convective @ 80 mps: v2_lower/central/upper_dr_turbine_equipment and both contribution columns are blank (extrapolation_flag=withheld_above_70_mps)

benchmark_value_damage_crosswalk_v2_proposed.csv: 25 of 27 usd_per_MW_physical_if_normalizable / pct_installed_TIV_if_normalizable cells remain blank (only the 2 value-ladder denominator rows normalize)

notes: This v0.6 addition is strictly ADDITIVE: it previews a staged, non-promoted v2.0 (docs r1) two-pathway candidate for wind_tornado_wind alongside the existing, unmodified v0.5 RESOLVED_FROM_DAMAGE_MODELING crosswalk. The v0.5 files listed under v1_0_files_checked are byte-unchanged.

v2 replaces the single shared D50-shift curve family (v1.0) with two independently governed pathways -- straight_line_convective and tornado_direct_hit -- sharing one turbine-equipment-assembly atom evaluated with mutually-exclusive ordered damage states, instead of v1.0's independent five-unit structural sum.

The value ladder itself (installed $1,968,000/MW; physical $1,623,000/MW; excluded $345,000/MW) is UNCHANGED and peril-agnostic -- it reconciles identically under both v1.0 and v2: 1090+239+294=1623; 1623+345=1968.

Every DR intensity-reference row and every recast benchmark row is tagged different_denominators_do_not_treat_as_regression_target where applicable, copied verbatim from the staged OLD_VS_NEW_COMPARISON source; withheld/inapplicable cells are blank, never zero.

The v2 candidate's promotion_gate.status is blocked: canonical use requires independent formula verification, bundle v3/emit v2/capability v3 schema review, all pathway KATs passing, Hazard-adapter migration to load a single pinned artifact, exposure/frequency/event-overlap seam corrections, and an explicit repository promotion decision. None of these gates have been passed; promotion: not_performed.

Row counts above equal the actual data-row counts of the new v2 CSVs (benchmark=27 matching the v1.0 benchmark row count exactly, intensity=24 = 12 speeds x 2 pathways) and the corresponding JSON array lengths.
